statusLine: EU fuel stocks remain under strain with 18 of 27 countries showing critical levels in at least one fuel category, though overall reserves stay above mandatory minimums.
fullAnalysis:
European fuel security faces significant pressure as regional tensions continue to drive oil prices higher and strain emergency stockpiles. Brent crude has surged 48.1% since late February to $107.33/barrel, forcing several countries to tap strategic reserves. Despite this stress, EU-wide averages remain above the mandatory 90-day minimum at 112.8 days for petrol, 101.5 days for diesel, and 95.1 days for jet fuel.

The distribution of reserves reveals concerning disparities across member states. Romania faces the most severe situation with all three fuel types at critical levels, including dangerously low diesel stocks at just 30.2 days. Poland and Luxembourg also show critical status across all categories, while major economies like Germany and Italy struggle with diesel and jet fuel shortages despite adequate petrol reserves. Aviation fuel presents the broadest challenge, with 17 of 27 countries at critical or warning levels.

Fuel prices reflect the underlying supply pressures, with EU averages reaching €1.817/L for petrol and €1.897/L for diesel. The Netherlands and Denmark face the highest petrol costs above €2.30/L, while Malta and Poland offer some relief with prices under €1.50/L. The current situation requires careful monitoring as geopolitical tensions persist and winter demand approaches.
keyPoints: 18 of 27 EU countries show critical fuel levels in at least one category, with Romania facing critical shortages across all three fuel types
Jet fuel stocks present the greatest regional vulnerability, with 17 countries at warning or critical levels and EU average at 95.1 days
Diesel supplies remain tight in major economies - Germany at 64.3 days, Italy at 74 days, both below the 90-day threshold
Oil prices have surged 48.1% since late February to $107.33/barrel, driving fuel costs to €1.817/L for petrol and €1.897/L for diesel
Regional price disparities are significant, with Netherlands petrol at €2.341/L compared to Malta's €1.34/L
